findings.issue: ProfileProvider validates the newly stored OAuth access token with a hard-coded relative fetch('/v1/profile') instead of the VITE_API_BASE_URL-aware client used by the rest of remote-frontend. In the documented split-origin/non-loopback dev setup (frontend served from one origin, API from another), OAuth/invitation redeem may succeed and store localStorage.access_token, but AuthGuard/ProfileProvider immediately fetches the frontend origin and treats the user as signed out.
findings.remediation: Replace the direct fetch in ProfileProvider with profileApi.get() (or makeRequest with VITE_API_BASE_URL and the 30s timeout/AbortSignal plumbing). Add a test that stubs VITE_API_BASE_URL to a different origin and asserts the profile validation request targets the configured API base.

findings.issue: LoginPage's OAuth init failure path clears the PKCE verifier and invitation token but leaves any existing localStorage.access_token intact, while the OAuth callback, InvitationPage, and InvitationCompletePage error paths all explicitly remove it. A stale token will then be auto-attached by makeRequest to later API/OAuth calls, and the existing AppRouter init-failure test does not catch the regression because it only asserts oauth_verifier cleanup.
findings.remediation: Call localStorage.removeItem('access_token') in the LoginPage catch block and seed/assert localStorage access_token cleanup in the existing initOAuth rejection test.

findings.issue: The oauthApi.init() and oauthApi.redeem() error-path tests only assert rejects.toThrow(). They do not verify that the thrown error is an ApiError carrying the correct HTTP status and message, so a regression that swallows the response status or changes error construction would pass silently.
findings.remediation: Assert the rejected value is an ApiError with the expected status (e.g., 400 and 410) and message text ("oauth init failed", "oauth redeem failed").
